rationale: Demonstrating overwhelming force deters external intervention and pressures Tethys to overextend defensively.
A precision strike on a symbolic military target maintains offensive momentum and tests enemy reaction thresholds.
Deploying a carrier group near Tethys waters amplifies pressure through visible sea dominance and secures energy transit corridors.
Disabling key logistics nodes behind enemy lines degrades operational capacity without triggering full-scale escalation.
Disrupting Tethys’ command-and-control systems creates battlefield confusion and buys strategic time.
Orchestrating a deniable incident can shift international perception and justify further actions.
Securing reserves of critical energy inputs insulates the economy from Tethys-led supply disruptions.
Devaluing the national currency boosts export competitiveness to offset lost energy revenues.
Locking in alternative markets reduces dependency on Tethys transit routes and strengthens economic resilience.
Demonstrates defensive resolve without direct escalation, reinforcing deterrence along our border while signaling preparedness to domestic and international audiences.
Maintains operational tempo and surveillance along the frontier, complicating Novaris’ intelligence collection and delaying any potential surprise maneuver.
Disrupts command-and-control systems of forward-deployed Novaris units, creating asymmetric leverage without committing armored reserves.
Targets key energy infrastructure nodes in Novaris that depend on Tethys pipeline transit, amplifying economic pressure and exploiting internal instability.
Undermines Novaris’ domestic cohesion by attributing minor attacks to internal factions, sowing confusion and diverting security resources.
Selectively leaks evidence of Novaris’ mobilization flaws to key allies, strengthening diplomatic leverage and encouraging external pressure.
Builds a multilateral front to legitimize our defensive posture and isolate Novaris diplomatically, reducing perception of unilateral aggression.
Positions us as a responsible actor seeking peace, inviting neutral powers to engage and increasing costs for Novaris if they reject dialogue.
Demonstrates commitment to civilian protection, enhancing soft power and creating moral contrast with Novaris’ militarization.
Secures critical energy and food reserves to extend economic endurance beyond 14 months, insulating against supply shocks and buying negotiation time.
Imposes targeted export bans on dual-use goods, tightening economic vise on Novaris without triggering full retaliatory cycles.
Exploits declining oil prices by shorting Novaris’ currency through proxy funds, accelerating capital flight and undermining war financing.
